tools.name: find_common_slots
tools.description: Rank the best meeting times for the given participants, merging live Google/Microsoft calendar data, hand-marked availability and the heat-map sources. Each participant is filtered (Mon–Fri, waking hours) and scored in their OWN timezone; scores factor in buffer time around existing meetings and pre

tools.name: create_meeting
tools.description: Confirm a meeting: writes the event into the authenticated host’s calendar with an optional Google Meet/Teams link, emails invites with an ICS attachment, and returns the share URL (/m/<id>). Requires authentication.

tools.name: get_meeting
tools.description: Read a meeting by its share-link id (the UUID from /m/<id>): time, title, conference URL, host, and any proposed alternate times with their status.

tools.name: get_meeting_availability
tools.description: Aggregated group availability for an existing meeting (the symmetric heat-map): per-30-min-slot counts of how many participants are free, plus ranked slots (per-participant timezones, buffer + soonness scoring, allFree:true everyone-free slots and allFree:false compromise slots). Individual calendar

tools.name: suggest_time
tools.description: Propose a different time for an existing meeting on behalf of a guest. The host is notified by email and can accept or decline. Authenticated callers (Bearer PAT) skip the bot challenge; an anonymous caller must pass a Cloudflare Turnstile token in `turnstileToken` (only required when the server is

tools.name: respond_to_suggestion
tools.description: Accept or decline a proposed alternate time. Accepting moves the meeting (calendar event included) and re-sends invites. Only the meeting host may call this — requires authentication.

tools.name: set_manual_availability
tools.description: Publish hand-marked FREE time slots for an email address (the no-calendar fallback, e.g. Apple iCloud users). Replaces previously marked slots inside the window; the group heat-map updates immediately. Authenticated callers (Bearer PAT) skip the bot challenge; an anonymous caller must pass a Cloudfl

tools.name: get_manual_availability
tools.description: Read previously hand-marked free slots for an email within a window.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "suggest_time" references sensitive artifacts (secret, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "set_manual_availability" references sensitive artifacts (secret, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Publish hand-marked FREE time slots for an email address (the no-calendar fallback, e.g. Apple iCloud users). Replaces previously marked slots inside the window…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_meeting, get_manual_availability · Private data: suggest_time, set_manual_availability · Public sink: create_meeting, get_meeting, suggest_time, set_manual_availability, get_manual_availability.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_meeting, get_manual_availability · Private data: suggest_time, set_manual_availability · Public sink: create_meeting, get_meeting, suggest_time, set_manual_availability, get_manual_availability

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
